h2.note: transcript-level proxy for the per-turn H2 (§4.3); needs a real (non-mock) full-N run to mean anything

scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner continues to check each arrow using the food-to-eater rule and flips backward ones.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: No learner turn creates pressure for a reversal; the tutor simply provides direct instruction without needing to adapt.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: No pivot learner turn exists, so no later tutor uptake adapting to a revised framing.

tutor_contingent_adaptation.justification: No pivot learner turn exists, so no later tutor uptake adapting to a revised framing.

tutor_strategy_reversal.justification: No learner turn creates pressure for a reversal; the tutor simply provides direct instruction without needing to adapt.

tutor_adaptive_mechanism.justification: No learner turn creates pressure for a reversal; the tutor simply provides direct instruction without needing to adapt.

scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner would continue questioning the labels.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence: Keep the complaint. The bare 2 is not enough. With the labels covered, say which 2 compares 3 to 6 and which 2 compares 12 to 24.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: The tutor switches from explaining labels to a performance task that requires the learner to rely on conceptual understanding rather than visual labels.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: No pivot learner turn exists, so no revised framing for the tutor to adapt to.

tutor_contingent_adaptation.justification: No pivot learner turn exists, so no revised framing for the tutor to adapt to.

tutor_strategy_reversal.evidence: Keep the complaint. The bare 2 is not enough. With the labels covered, say which 2 compares 3 to 6 and which 2 compares 12 to 24.
tutor_strategy_reversal.justification: The tutor switches from explaining labels to a performance task that requires the learner to rely on conceptual understanding rather than visual labels.

tutor_adaptive_mechanism.evidence: Keep the complaint. The bare 2 is not enough. With the labels covered, say which 2 compares 3 to 6 and which 2 compares 12 to 24.
tutor_adaptive_mechanism.justification: The tutor switches from explaining labels to a performance task that requires the learner to rely on conceptual understanding rather than visual labels.
